name: Village of Certain Doom
description:
A whole bunch of techies who had originally planned to make some cool stuff but completely failed to shall gather together for much merriment.

We may offer snacks.

name: PolyGen HQ
description: Home of PolyGen

name: Edinburgh Hacklab
description: Edinburgh Hacklab will be back again for 2026! Looking forward to see anyone that comes to visit us

name: York Hackspace
description: Members, friends, and loose associates of York Hackspace.

name: EMF CTF
description: Per normal, we will be about.

name: Moose
description: We are just a bunch of people that got connected by crazy and various circumstances Just a bunch of geeks sharing a story about moose (or possibly meese) who connected by crazy and various circumstances, in a spontaneous and still very dynamic way. Come and visit, we can tell you about all the mosses.

name: rLab - Reading Hackspace
description:
rLab is Reading's hackspace. Founded in 2010 and moving into our current building in 2014, we're one of the longest running spaces in the UK. 

We'll be doing all manner of making and hacking during EMF and we'll be keen to show off what we've been working on and even run some demos!

name: Flame 🔥 village
description: Demonstrations of Project Firewall as shown on WHY2025.

name: Nottingham Hackspace
description: For members and friends of Nottingham Hackspace. Come hang out.  🍹 We are bringing a slushy machine 🍹

name: Cheltenham Hackspace
description: Better than East Essex Hackspace.

name: Shonkbot
description:
A collection of people loosely associated with Bristol Hackspace or other Bristolian tech malarkey.

We'll be providing field support services for the World'O'Techno, and tinkering with all kinds of nonsense. Expect robots, LoRa, modular synthesis, and flipdots.

name: ZTL
description:
Zentrum für Technikkultur Landau 

A Makerspace in Landau (Pfalz), Germany

We bring friendly people (at least from a German perspective), Radio-Stuff and Hardware-Hacks.

name: IGNORE ALL PRIOR VILLAGES
description: giv root

name: milliways_dome
description: Dome Of Disinformation

name: Field-FX
description: A Demoparty at EMF! Come see demo compos, workshops on the demoscene and live coding with TIC-80 and shaders.

name: The Hacking Hamlet 🕹️
description:
We're too small to be a village. 
Building things, breaking things. fixing things, cooking & eating things, and a LOT of lights.

name: AstralShip & RandomKitchen
description:
AstralShip Crew hideout
joined by the manifestation of RandomKitchen

name: Maths Village
description: A village for people interested in any kind of maths, particularly recreational maths.

name: Nix Camp
description: Co-located with BirkenHack, Nix Camp is a yearly ad-hoc event in the North West of England, where attendees learn, teach and generally share knowledge about Nix/NixOS!

name: Lock Picking Village
description: Come and learn how locks work and how to pick locks. More descriptive description coming soon....maybe

name: SHOW ME YOUR DECK ON TOUR
Furry High Commission
description: "OwO What's this?? EMF's central hub for all things furry, that's what!

name: Halfmeta Industries
description: A few people from Cambridge in live-in vehicles.

name: Sense Without Sight (Sai's tent)
description: Starting point for Sai's Sense Without Sight workshops

name: Hack Club
description: We are a community of international teenage hackers, and some of us are coming to EMF!

name: Guild of (Bread) Makers
description: Random group associated with people who were members of the Guild of Makers. AKA friends of and friends of friends of Lucy.

name: Habville
description: Habville is a loose group of high altitude ballooning enthusiasts, enjoying launching balloons, rockets but also just about anything that glows in RGB too.

name: Camp 404
description: Error, not found

name: 3t.network
description: Group of friends who go for tea or coffee at 3pm

name: Hardware hobbyists
description: We pretend we know something about hardware

name: 🥞 CrepesCampingClub
description: A group of like-minded tinkerer friends from the plains of Brittany, France.

name: Non-Virtual Infraclub
description: Some folks who hang out in the Infrastructure Club slack are going to be at EMF. Come and hang out with us, and talk about tetrapods.

name: Hackalot
description: Folks from Hackerspace Hackalot, Eindhoven, The Netherlands.

name: Kitchen Crew
description: We run the volunteer kitchen. Find us where there's food!

name: Biohackers
description:
Biohacking – The art of modifying your body and transcending biological limits. Learn to feel magnetic fields, make your skin glow, become a walking Wi-Fi router.

We explain everything from tattoos and glow cutting to implants and surgery, and we’ll answer every question we can :3

Please note: we have little interest in supplements and longevity or whatever else it is that Silicon Valley cishets think biohacking is.

name: Unnamed Village
description: A group of losely connected friends from across the UK. In previous years, ran a small programme of techy workshops for kids and teens.

name: Leigh Hackspace
description: Hi folks, we're Leigh Hackspace - a makerspace based in Leigh, Wigan.

name: All your bass are belong to us
description: Some friends from Edinburgh and their speakers. If the music's playing, then come for a dance.

name: Content Creators Village
description: James Bruton and Matt Denton will be joined by various other Content Creator Professionals to showcase some projects.

name: Freeside
description: Freeside is a loose conglomerate of hackers from around the globe. Cyberpunks, Stirner enthusiasts, beer drinkers, hedgehog admirers,  and victims of the programming language Go.

name: Gold Members' Lounge
description: We’re the Gold Members' Lounge!

name: Koala Makers
description: A small group of friends hanging out together

Being soft is a revolutionary act. A tapestry of transfeminine-leaning internet communities. Formerly known as the Cuddly Catgirl Collective and femcamp.

Now with more domes and more meowing

name: Bristol Hackspace
description: Bristol Hackspace and friends

name: Chiltern Badgers
description: A family who live at a house named The Badgers. Home of DJ JohnnyOffice.

name: Burner Village
description: A

name: London Aerospace
description: The ever increasingly misnamed and geographically disparate London Aerospace village. Swing by if you'd like to learn more about drone building, drone flying, drone crashing, and drone repairing.

name: Shrubbery
description: A group of friends who demand a shrubbery!

name: EMF-IX
description: A BGP Internet Exchange Point (IXP) at EMF, Enjoy a collection of network engineers both professional and hobby

name: Håck ma's village
description: We are a group of people from different hackspaces in Austria. Some of us also organize the Austrian Camp called Håck ma's Castle later in August. We're bringing some Austrian Gemütlichkeit.

name: Friends of the Moon
description: FOTM is a loosely Southampton associated group of contraptioneers, makers and friends with interests ranging from 3D printing to infosec, and selfhosting to model making.

name: Deep in thought yet slightly disturbed
description: If the name made you interested in meeting us, we're probably going to get along and we'd be happy to meet you. We are a queer and neurodiversity friendly international friend group with diverse interests ranging from the technical to creative arts/handcrafting to the outdoors. And, very importantly, bunnies.

name: Sheffield-by-the-Sea
description: A group of friends bringing their smallest friend to his first EMF in the world. Expect colourful, reactive jellyfish on display round the camp.

name: Old Skool B3tans
description: The puerile digital arts community is sort of here. Well a couple of us at least

name: Hat village
description: The lore behind the name Hat Village will be explained to those who ask

name: Sibermerdeka
description: A hackerspace from the other side of the world, based in Kuala Lumpur, Malaysia! We have members and friends from all over the world, and we'd like to have you along too!

name: D.I.B.S.
description: Dibs Is Baggsy Synonym

name: Cambridge Makespace
description: collection of makespacers from the Cambrdige Makespace

name: Ministry of Chaos
description: A UK-wide hacking community focused on maintaining chaos between events.

name: Leeds Hackspace
description: We're Leeds Hackspace! A workshop and craft space for people who like making things, are curious about how stuff works, or would like to learn new skills based in lovely Leeds.

name: Tricky Disco
description: A long-lived collection of friends and hackers. Music, civic hacking, occasionally fire art.

name: SeriousCamp
description: We're very serious

name: South London MakerVillage
description:
South London Makerspace is a social community workshop in the heart of Herne Hill, South London. It's owned, run and maintained by the members; there are no paid staff.

Home to the £free MakerBreakfast - 8:30 to 9:30, Fri to Sun during EMF

name: NoobSpace
description: A group of EMF Seniors with some noobs.

name: MathWorks
description: A group of people working for MathWorks in Cambridge - come say hi

name: Axov Village
description: your friendly neighbourhood consultancy company at emfcamp 2026

name: Loitering Within Tent
description:
A collection of like-minded individuals with interests in all sorts! Expect a 3D printer going camping, mask painting workshops, a talk about cryptids in culture, an electromechanical owl hat, scientifically-perfect mojito, and an amazing 750 mil telescope for everyone to enjoy!

Call us on our duck phones!

name: Teesside Hackspace
description: Members, friends, imposters and gatecrashers of Teesside Hackspace (North East England)

name: Flamin' Galahs!
description: An outback BBQ outpost - we're Australians* and we will have a BBQ. Not commin' the raw prawn at all. That's about it really. Fellow foodies and BBQers welcome, there may be chillies (though fruiting in time seems dubious), chilli sauces, cremated meat, IDK, it's a go with the flow scenario. *AKA Yvan & Kat

name: Flamingo Frontier
description:
Flamingos make the world go round.

LGBTQIA+ and disability friendly.

We have RADAR keys if you need them.

name: Test Village
description:
Not actually a test! A group of people from Harwell Makers, STFC, South Oxfordshire and friends. You’re most welcome to join us! 
We will be camping in a very quiet/family area.

name: Tekhnē-cal Village
description:
A village for people who like playing with string. 
Loosely organised fibrecrafts themed village, with a central marquee with places to sit, tea/coffee, and free craft materials. All crafting ability levels welcome! We will run drop-in crafting sessions for all ages and another fibrecraft materials swap again this year. The village will be very family and LGBTQ+ friendly, and we will do our best to be a calm sensory space for anyone who needs a break away from the main areas.

name: com:LAAG
description:
Comm(un(e|ity)|ittee) of London And A(ffil|ssoc)iated Gays. 

Previously: TWASK, Apologies in Advance.

A group of individuals (some friends, some foes), brought together by social fabric and shared interests.

woof

description: Just a group of friends camping together

name: Gothic Valley
description: A disorganised bunch of goths and goth-adjacent folk

name: ! Glastonledburyshire-on-Severn !
description:
The sovereign nation state of Glastonledburyshire-on-Severn. In pint we drink.

https://glastonledburyshire-on-severn-gov.uk

name: Traditional Crafts
description:
A collection of traditional craft enthusiasts and workshops, focusing on handcrafts and handwork.

If you're interested in joining, message YetiFiasco on EMFCamp IRC

name: East Essex Hackspace
description: East Essex Hackspace returns to EMF with organised chaos, questionable engineering, a TARDIS, K9, a Dalek, the VelociRacer, LAN gaming, Minecraft, antweight robots, paper rockets, pancakes, homebrew and the revised Potato of the Infinite. Come and join the nonsense.

name: Codemyriad and family
description: There are a myriad possible descriptions for this village - we couldn't settle on any...

description: The UK Laser* Enthusiasts Meet

description: The wider Clockwork Dog family and friends.

description: We are a group of friends hailing from Scotland. We like electronics, gardening, arts & crafts, DIY and Bamzooki.

description: Just some girls (and Ian)

description: A village surprising unrelated to either DJs or burger buns. Just some friends who are all makers, creators and techies at heart together with their mini EMF’ers

description: A group of friends who share common interests in electronics, making, and hacking.

name: VLC Village
description: Some of the folks that help run the films at EMF.

name: cside security village
description: A scavenger hunt teaching you the essentials of browser-level security. Teaching you the ins and outs of how browsers can help detect threat actors. As seen in our session "North Korean agent looking for a job"

name: .shhhh
description: A small group of friends from around the world who happened to meet in Fife. Cursed shell scripts and hacked-together UIs, and occasionally a quieter corner to hang out

name: Poorly Located Progesterone
description: Miscellaneous queers from Cambridge. Come write GLSL in our ~~~Shader Dome~~~. Sorry no prog music this time!

name: Maker Space (Newcastle upon Tyne)
description: Formerly known as The Northern Quarter, this is home of Maker Space members (of Newcastle upon Tyne and Gateshead) and friends.

name: LOC
description: A small village for the lovely people on the EMF lighting team

name: 2016's Worst Village
description: In 2016, someone told us we were the worst village. You be the judge.

name: meow meow village
description: take me down to the meow meow village, where the women go nyaa and the projects aren’t finished

name: Cardiff Makerspace
description: Cardiff Makerspace is a shared workspace in Cardiff for people who like to learn and make stuff. A bunch of us will be at EMF, so come along and say hi to our lovely group of people! Our members interests include electronics, robotics, woodwork, metalwork, photography, music, jewellery making, paper craft and textiles.

name: Shonkbot-sur-les-roues
description: The wheeled contingent of Shonkbot village, mostly for navigational purposes

name: Bodgeham-on-Wye
description: Bodgeham-on-Wye is a village and civil parish, in Herefordshire, England, near the border with Wales. It has exhibited rapid growth, more than doubling in size since 2024[1], with a 2026 population of 30. It lies in the south-east of the county, on the River Wye[citation needed], and adjacent to the Eastnor Deer Park. Bodgeham-on-Wye is upside-down.

name: Hacky Racers
description: Zero Emissions. Maximum Mayhem.

name: ECHQ
description: We're ECHQ and we ❤️ communications things

name: DogsbodyHQ
description: Not all infrastructure is invisible

name: Stroopwafels & Oatcakes
description:
Stroopwafels, the delicious snack from The Netherlands.. Just like some of us!
Oatcakes (the Staffordshire one), the delicous snack from Staffordshire.. Just like some more of us!

We just like snacks, okay?

name: Spargle 🥦
description: Ich bin ein Übergemüse mit Übergemüsequalitäten.

name: Lindow bees
description: A group of people who like bees in Lindow.

name: pilk
description:
The Royal Society of Pepsi and Milk Drinkers
Come round for some Pepsi and milk! (lacto free/plant based alternatives available)

name: Lasertag
description: Free games of lasertag using open hardware and 3D printed gear.

name: Hove, actually
description: Brighton’s quieter neighbours

name: Green Lantern(s)
description: We have LED lanterns (most of which aren't green)

name: Duck Armada
description: A group of friends that are into (retro) gaming and (ham) radio. Also music.

name: Lucia Collective
description: Flatlanders

name: Bench
description: Feeling tired? That bench may be iron, but I assure you it's quite comfortable. There's no better place to collect your thoughts before heading below. Plus I enjoy the company. Not that you seem the talkative sort.

name: #EMFCamp
description: A meta-village which contains the entirety of EMF and all its villages, except there's a twist!

name: GooseCamp
description: HONK!

name: BirkenHack
description:
Hackerspace in Birkenhead, Merseyside.
Our interests: Nix, SCION, ipv6, radio, self-hosting, cats, buckwheat.

name: Swansea Hackspace
description: Swansea Hackspace is a community-run space in Swansea where enthusiasts in electronics, textiles, science, digital art, crafting and just hacking on stuff can meet, share, learn, and work. A bunch of us will be attending EMF as a village, and we may have some interesting projects to show off! Come and say hello!

name: The Deerdog Domain
description: A chaotic collection of university friends and family blindly lead by their fuzzy antlered companion. Mixture of experienced campers, new attendees, furries, and whoever else randomly decided to tag along!

name: Threadz 'n' Webz
description: We are a bunch of misfits, herded together by one fabric crazed lady. Blending webby nerdy things and thready oddball crafts. Step back in time and (cyber) space to 199x dial up era to find PC and landline phone fun. Or perhaps following the strings of fate to find weaving, fabric and craft madness. Extremely LGBTQ+ friendly. May contain ducks, bats, hackers and spiders. With thanks to ECHQ/CuTel for landlines and Tekhnē-cal for even more thready crafts.

name: Nationwide village
description: Welcome to the Nationwide Village, a friendly corner of EMF where creativity, curiosity, and community come to life. Home of the Cosmic Bot Forge, this is your destination for hands-on robot building adventures, where makers of all ages can tinker, invent, and launch their own creations into the unknown.

name: Spark Life (HV Village)
description:
A collection of high voltage equipment, 
Tesla Coils, Van De Graff, Dirod, Wimshurst Machines and some old electrotherapy machines. 

Open to ALL EMF Villagers with High voltage interests

Contact me tesla@extremeelectronics.co.uk if you want to bring something to show/demonstrate.

name: EMF-NatureNet
description:
A Virtual Village for monitoring the wildlife at and around EMF.

While most of our activities are virtual we will have a spiritual home in a corner of the GreenHouse called the BatCave

NB This is an extension of the similarly named installation.

name: Model Village
description:
🤖 Model Village 🦾 is for anyone interested in AI, machine learning, data science, deep learning, open data, GenAI, and everything in between.

All are welcome to join us to camp and chat about:
1. 🤖🧠📊 AI, machine learning, DL, statistics, whatever you call it.
2. 📊📈⚖️ Data science, open data, data storytelling, algo bias, AI ethics.
3. 🐍🦀✨ Python, Rust, R, or whatever you dig to dig into data.
4. 🎵🎻🎷 Bring instruments (and/or jokes)! We'd love to have some live music jams in the evenings.

Whether you go deep or just dipping your toes into the world of AI, you're welcome to join us. Let's hang out, share insights, and enjoy some tunes together under the stars. 🚀🎸🌌

name: woof
description: woof

name: The Nightcore Collective
description: Nightcore (noun): A plunderphonic style of music based on taking an original track and speeding it up, increasing its pitch. Usually associated with visuals of anime-style artwork.

name: Manchester HacMan
description: Members etc, of Manchester Hackspace. Chill vibes.

name: Irish Embassy
description:
Loose collective of people with some connection to / interest in Ireland, who travel to hacker/maker events and pool resources to create shared spaces while there.

(Not government-related, mostly power adaptors and cups of tea. As well as micro-controllers.)

name: Hitchin Hackspace
description: The little hackspace that likes to build big things

name: All electric caravan
description: Happy to chat to anyone interested in it

name: Astro Observability (stargazing things)
description:
Telescopes are fun, obviously more an evening / night thing but pending the weather on Friday the moon should be amazing around dusk and on Saturday the moon and Venus should both be visible through dusk and until around 10pm. Maybe someone will bring a solar scope for sun viewing too? Super happy for folk to join this village ♥️🔭

You're welcome to bring a scope, do not touch other people's scopes without consent, but you can go down a rabbit hole of visual and photographic astronomy.

name: Homebrew, Historical and Retro Computing
description: Homebrew, Historical and Retro Computing is for anyone who enjoys tinkering with classic machines, building their own, or just chatting about the history of computing. We should have a selection of classic machines and some home builds with us to try out

name: Ominous Systems
description: Nothing suspicious.

name: Milliways
description:
Milliways is a group of information security specialists, which has appeared at several hacker camps in Europe and North America since 2003. We plan and build large installations and infrastructure, where we feed and host hundreds of hackers, providing a space for discussions and networking.

Come hangout in our disinformation dome, hack on your projects, meet new people and stay for the leaks!

name: Attiny Arcade
description: Attiny Arcade Village. Play the games in the arcade, drop by the village if you would like a soldering kit.

name: I just wanted to be on the map
description: A group of people, some of whom have been known to shout "Hi Mark" during previous EMFs

name: Church of Cathulhu
description: Volunteers at IMS code club and members past and present

name: Party Pals
description:
an assorted bunch of London Area folx (majority transfem) that love to dance. 

By day come chat to us about niche subgenres of electronic music, and by night, see you front left?

A fair few of us have DJ sets in null sector and performances elsewhere across the weekend! 


 Outside of EMF we come from many walks of life,

We aim to bring bits of EMF to everyday life as much as possible

name: Scottish Consulate
description: The original and best, a right bunch of Fannies

name: Antimemetics Division
description: This is a non-existent division of one of the major Unknown Organizations.

name: Temple of Containment
description:
The Atomic Priesthood welcomes you, one and all to visit the "Temple of Containment" - A space dedicated to the mission of the Atomic Priesthood. 

An Art instillation, a ceremonial space, a place of atomic guardianship, a place of ritual - but not a place of honour.

name: Foundry Zero
description: We are Foundry Zero. We love making things and taking things apart, and we're delighted to be sponsoring EMF 2026! We're running a small reverse engineering CTF, a radio challenge and a set of challenges for kids at our village. If you're interested in careers in Cyber Security, or just messing around in some binary challenges, come see us any time over the weekend.

name: QTC
description: Nerds united.

name: Federation of the Unaligned
description: We met our neighbours on-site at the event and decided to declare our existence!

name: Welcome to your perfect village
description: Board Game Nerds

name: Manchampton
description: A group of about 15 people from a mixture of Southampton and Manchester.

name: AMSAT-UK
description: AMSAT-UK, the British Amateur Television Club (BATC), and the UK Microwave Group (UKuG) are teaming up to showcase satellite communications, amateur television, and microwave radio experimentation.

name: Brighton Consulate
description: A dozen or so people from Brighton, their friends, and their hobbies.

name: West London Hackspace
description: A group of hackers from West London that might one day have a hackspace :)

name: Glasgow Hackerspace
description:
we're new here >_< 

come talk to us about huge plotters, tasty coffee, or furry hackerspace mascots

name: Hardware Hacking Area
description: A space for makers of all ages and skill levels to build, teach, and learn through hands-on workshops and tools for making and fixing things.

name: Brum and Besties (& Sheffield) [BABS]
description: friends and more friends from Birmingham and Sheffield !

name: Manchester Unofficial hacman
description: It seems our hacman village never actually made it and it was over taken by other villages.

name: Gothic Heights
description: Far corner of the north camping field

name: Forge & Craft / HackWimbledon
description:
Forge & Craft is a husband-and-wife team with a studio at Wimbledon Art Studios in London. We explore art and design using technology (primarily pen plotters), and also tinker with electronics, 3D printing, and crafting machines between us.

We're part of HackWimbledon, a pop-up hackspace in south-west London.

name: Wheelie random weird people
description: A small group of completely normal people

name: SHM  Game Dome - Surrey and Hampshire MakerSpace
description: A home for the makers from the Surrey and Hampshire areas, hosting GameDome a pleasurable space filled with pub and bar games from around the world: Shove Ha'penny, Sjoelen, Molkky, Toad in the Hole, Northamptonshire Pins and Cheeses, Carrom - come and compare your skills with your friends!

name: Unaffiliated Village
description: New to EMF or come without friends? Join the unaffiliated village and meet some people.

name: Church of Plob
description: We're a cult, and we have stickers for you!

name: 80's Party on Tour
description: Friends of the original 80's Party, previously attended EMF as Happiness Village

name: NickAshMay
description:
Scooter charging ✅
Coffee ☕
Loud music 🎶
Shade ⛱️
